Mission services Will be held
next Sunday in the Ondawa Inn,
which has been purchased by the
North Coast railroad as part of
the union station site. The place
was closed last week.


Attorney n. M. Stephens has
about concluded the negotiations
for the sale of the northeast cor
ner of Second avenue and Stew
ems, owned by him, to J. H. Spear,
for 100000. The ground fronts
10 feet on Second avenue and 142
on Stevens and is improved with
a three story brick building occu-
pied by the Waverly hotel


P. r. Pyman mas taken out a
building permit for a $4,000 brick
building on Short avenue near
Bernard street.


Announcement Is made that the
Western Union has adopted the
new rule of accepting night mes
sages or letters, as they are call
ed, of so words or under at the
same rate as day messages.
At a meeting Of prominent men,
bers of the 10000 club yesterday
afternoon, it was decided to try
to enlist the efforts of congress
men west of the Mississippi river
in bringing about the passage of
the bill providing for the increase
| in pay of rural mail carriers to 500.
The bin also includes a provision
for $3.50 extra for every mile over
"a in the route and a subtraction
of $3.50 for every mile under that
number.


John Anderson, accused of abus-
! ing his wife, was given the severest
penalty the law could enforce, in
the police court yesterday after.
noon by Judge Mann. Anderson is
said to have attempted to assault
| his wife. while he was in drunken
rage, She ran from him and in so
doing fell in front of a street car
and broke her leg.


Frank and Madge King, who ran
a matrimonial confidence bureau at
/ the AVOndale court, were fined $50
each in the police court yesterday
by Justice Mann. The Kings had
succeeded in playing cupid quite

extensively.


The Spokane women who won
gold and bronze medals at the dis
play in the Woman's building al
the Alaska-YukOn-Pacific eXpOsi
/ ton at Seattle last summer, have
just received their medals. The
women of this county carried away
4 good per cent of the prizes fol
their clever work. The gold med
ais have not as yet arrived.


The North Central high school
students are rapidly gaining a rep
utation as debaters. April Is the
North Central school will debate
with the Occur d'Alene high school
and on March % will debate
against the Hillyard high school.


The mayor yesterday made the


statement that he will submit the
1 draft of q commission form of gov


eminent prepared by the charter


revision committee to the city


council at its regular meeting two


weeks from tonight.


In a report to the mayor on the
reason for the collapse of the
Monroe street bridge bridge, City


Engineer Ralston blames it on the


sliding earth fill at the south end


of the bridge. lie says the over
/ loading of the bridge with new


steel was not the cause, as there


was only % pounds to the square


foot when the bridge fell, as


against 40 pounds at one time. Yer


in prior statement Engineer Raj
ston said that he had known the
bridge was unsafe for many


months and that it was barely
changing by it's bootheels."
